title: 控方证人
summary:
Sir Arlington West engages in a discussion with Mrs. Everly regarding the causes of harm and the nature of hereditary diseases. Using color blindness to illustrate the mechanics of gender-limited inheritance, Sir Arlington notes that madness is inherited with equal probability in both males and females. During this conversation, Claire Trent becomes visibly pale and distressed, pleading with the group to stop the discussion before the arrival of Mrs. Thompson. Observing Claire's anxiety and the tension held by Jack Trent, Dermot West feels like an outsider to a secret shared among the group.

The gathering moves to the living room for a séance led by Mrs. Thompson, a medium who uses the Japanese name Kitsune. A spirit named Tsukimi speaks through the medium, recounting mundane details of her life. However, a second, more sinister voice emerges, issuing a frantic warning to one of the three men present: "Don't go home. Danger! Blood!" Dermot feels this warning is directed specifically at him. After the session, a weary Mrs. Thompson remarks that the room feels filled with the "scent of death" and asks if anyone has experienced recent sudden deaths.

Following the medium's departure, Sir Arlington declines an invitation to dance and asks to speak with Dermot privately. As they walk toward Harley Street, Sir Arlington apologizes for taking Dermot away and instructs him to meet the others at the Grafton Gallery. While searching his pockets for his keys, Sir Arlington instructs Dermot to ring the bell for Johnson.
